title: Meet The Explorers
big_question: Làm thế nào để nói mình giỏi làm gì và đồ vật này của ai bằng tiếng Anh nhỉ?
grammar: Be good at / not good at + V-ing: I'm good at (playing football). / I'm not good at (climbing trees).
Possessive apostrophe 's: Mike is Tom's uncle. / It's John's bike.
phonics: Short vowel sounds: a, e, i, o, u (e.g. cat, pet, bin, dog, tub)
skills: Talking about abilities (what people can or can't do well)
Talking about family relationships and possessions
Reviewing numbers 21-100 and locations
project: Draw and talk about your family tree
speaking_prompts: What are you good at doing?
Are you good at playing the guitar?
Where is the cellar?
Who is this? It's my aunt.

mini_games.prompt: This is a room under the ground in a castle or a house.
I'm really good at _______ (do) puzzles.
This is my _______ (brother) guitar.

title: Our school
big_question: Có những môn học nào ở trường nhỉ?
grammar: Like / love / don't like + V-ing: I like listening to music. / He loves learning about Science.
Have to / has to + infinitive (Obligation): You have to wear a school uniform. / She has to do her homework.
phonics: Letter names: Spelling abbreviations (e.g. P.E., I.T., OK, UK, USA, DJ)
skills: Reading, listening and speaking about school subjects and daily rules
Carrying out a survey and making a bar chart about favourite subjects
project: Make an insect with geometrical shapes
speaking_prompts: What's your favourite subject?
When have you got English?
What are you good at?
Do you like doing number puzzles?

mini_games.prompt: You learn about numbers and shapes in this subject.
We _______ to wear a school uniform.
She really loves _______ (listen) to music.

title: The picnic
big_question: Có đồ ăn gì ở đây?
grammar: Questions and answers with some and any: Is there any cheese? / There are some oranges.
Making suggestions: Shall we make some soup? / How about some tea?
phonics: The sounds /ɪ/ and /aɪ/: e.g. fish, pink vs. pie, bike, ice cream
skills: Listening, reading and speaking about food, meals and healthy eating
Role-playing ordering food and drink at a sandwich shop
clil_topic: Science: Edible plants (roots, stems, leaves, seeds)
project: Draw a picnic with food and drink from plant parts
speaking_prompts: Would you like some lemonade?
How about some cheese?
Shall we make a pizza?
Are there any apples in your bag?

mini_games.prompt: This is a sweet drink made from yellow fruit.
Is there _______ soup in the bowl?
_______ we have a cheese sandwich for lunch?

title: Daily tasks
big_question: Đọc giờ một cách 'sang chảnh' hơn, bạn đã biết chưa?
vocabulary: take the dog for a walk
grammar: Telling the time: It's quarter past / half past / quarter to / o'clock.
Adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes, never): I always tidy up on Saturdays. / He never goes to bed early.
phonics: Voiced and unvoiced consonants /v/ and /f/: e.g. van, fan
skills: Listening and reading for specific information about daily routines and times
Writing a diary or a poem about daily tasks and family routines
project: Choose a job that people do at night. Write a diary for their day or night.
speaking_prompts: What time do you get up?
Do you usually wash up after dinner?
I always take the dog for a walk in the morning.
What's your favourite job at home?

mini_games.prompt: You do this with a broom to clean the floor.
I _______ get up at seven o'clock every day.
It's quarter _______ nine (8:45).

title: Around town
big_question: Chúng ta có thể tìm thấy gì ở các thị trấn nhỉ?
grammar: Prepositions of place (near, opposite, below, above, next to, between): The bank is opposite the library.
Be going to + infinitive of purpose: I'm going to the library to get a book.
phonics: The sounds /ɑː/ and /r/: e.g. car, park, far, robot
skills: Listening for directions and identifying places in a town
Writing and answering text messages about where you are going
project: Imagine you are in a town. Write text messages about where you are at different times.
speaking_prompts: Where's the cinema?
It's opposite the park.
Where are you going?
I'm going to the supermarket to buy some food.

mini_games.prompt: You go to this place to read or borrow books.
The car park is _______ to the supermarket.
I'm going to the sports centre _______ play tennis.

title: Under the sea
big_question: Dưới biển ngày xưa có những gì nhỉ?
grammar: Past simple of 'to be' (was / were): I was scared! / We were at the beach. / There were dolphins in the sea.
Questions and answers with was / were: Were you in the sea, Sue? No, I wasn't. / Was Tim on the beach? Yes, he was.
phonics: The letter sounds /s/ and /ʃ/ (sh): e.g. sea, shell, scared, shark
skills: Reading, listening and speaking about sea creatures and past events
Writing a description of a sea creature
clil_topic: Environmental studies: People and the sea (Climate change and sea pollution)
project: Make a leaflet to stop climate change and sea pollution
speaking_prompts: Where were you yesterday?
Were you on the beach?
What was there in the sea?
There were two big dolphins.

mini_games.prompt: This fish has a head like a horse.
There _______ two big dolphins in the sea yesterday.
Was there a shark? No, there _______.

title: Gadgets
big_question: So sánh các đồ vật như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Comparatives: My phone is smaller than yours. / Your phone's more beautiful than mine.
Superlatives: This is the fastest car in the world. / This is the most expensive house in the world.
phonics: Long vowel sounds with split digraphs (magic 'e'): e.g. cave, lime, game, time
skills: Reading and listening for specific information about gadgets and their uses
Writing an advertisement for a gadget
project: Make a cave painting from life today
speaking_prompts: Which gadget is your favourite?
My tablet is bigger than your mobile phone.
Is it more expensive than a laptop?
This is the most beautiful watch.

mini_games.prompt: You use this gadget to see in the dark.
My laptop is _______ (expensive) than your mobile phone.
This is the _______ (fast) train in the world.

title: In the hospital
big_question: Điều gì giúp chúng ta luôn khỏe mạnh nhỉ?
grammar: Past simple (regular verbs): jump -> jumped, shout -> shouted, land -> landed, look -> looked.
Past simple (irregular verbs): wake up -> woke up, feel -> felt, have -> had, go -> went, give -> gave, say -> said.
phonics: The sounds /t/, /d/ and /ɪd/ in '-ed' past simple endings (e.g. jumped, played, landed)
skills: Reading and listening for specific information about health and past events
Writing a story in the past tense
project: Make a storyboard about keeping healthy
speaking_prompts: What's the matter?
I've got a headache.
Where were you yesterday?
Yesterday I woke up at eight.

mini_games.prompt: You go to see this person at the hospital when you are ill.
Yesterday, she _______ (go) to the doctor's.
I've got a pain in my ear. I've got an _______.

title: Around the world
big_question: Trên thế giới có những kỳ quan nào nhỉ?
grammar: Negatives with past simple: We went to the beach, but we didn't go swimming.
Questions and answers with past simple: Did you go shopping? Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
Wh- questions in past simple: When did you get home? / Where did you go?
phonics: The sounds /i:/ and /ɪ/ (Different pronunciations of the letter 'y': e.g. jelly, gym, sky)
skills: Listening and reading about different countries and holidays
Speaking and writing about a country and past holiday events
project: Make a poster about a country
speaking_prompts: Did you go to Spain?
Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
Where did you go on holiday?
How long did you stay there?

mini_games.prompt: This is a big country where you can see kangaroos.
We _______ (not go) to the beach yesterday.
_______ you have a good holiday? Yes, I did.

title: Holiday plans
big_question: Các kỳ nghỉ khác nhau như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Future with 'be going to' + infinitive (Plans and predictions): I'm going to play in the garden all day. / It's going to be rainy on Tuesday.
Questions and answers with 'be going to': Are you going to cook pizza? Yes, I am. / What are you going to do? I'm going to visit my cousins.
phonics: The long vowel sound /ɜː/ with alternative spellings (er, ir, ur, ear, or): e.g. perfect, bird, Thursday, nurse, earth
skills: Listening and reading for specific information about holidays and weather forecasts
Talking about future holiday plans and weather predictions
Writing an email about a holiday
project: Make a storyboard about a holiday in the past
speaking_prompts: What's the weather going to be like tomorrow?
It's going to be sunny and hot.
What are you going to do on holiday?
I'm going to visit my grandparents.

mini_games.prompt: You wear this coat when it rains to keep your clothes dry.
I _______ going to play tennis tomorrow.
_______ you going to pack your bags? Yes, I am.
